days.focus: Safety, belonging, entering the room, and learning how our classroom works.

blocks.subject: Morning Work
blocks.title: If I Were an Animal Today
blocks.objective: Students independently complete a low-risk drawing and writing response while practicing arrival expectations.
steps: Model where backpacks, water bottles, folders, and supplies belong.
Direct students to begin the animal-feelings page.
Circulate and learn names while students work.

blocks.subject: Community
blocks.title: Get to Know You: Four Corners
blocks.objective: Students learn classmates’ interests while practicing listening, movement, personal space, and respectful responses.
steps: Explain that each corner represents one answer.
Invite students from different corners to share.

blocks.subject: Read Aloud
blocks.title: If I Built a School
blocks.objective: Students listen actively, discuss creative school ideas, and connect the story to their own classroom community.
steps: Complete the My Dream School poem orally or in writing.
resources: Read Aloud - If I Built a School.pdf

blocks.subject: Writing Workshop
blocks.title: Lesson 1: What Will Writing Workshop Look and Sound Like?
blocks.objective: Students identify expectations for the mini lesson, independent work time, and sharing time.
steps: Explain the three parts of writing workshop.
Practice a brief independent writing period and partner share.

blocks.subject: Procedures
blocks.title: Arrival, Carpet, Attention Signal, and Restroom
blocks.objective: Students correctly demonstrate the four highest-priority classroom procedures.
steps: Ask students to identify what needs to change.
Repeat until the class can perform it successfully.

prep: Choose and label the Four Corners
Post arrival and carpet anchor charts

days.focus: Materials, transitions, partnerships, and caring for classroom tools.

blocks.subject: Morning Work
blocks.title: Independent Arrival Practice
blocks.objective: Students repeat the complete arrival routine with less teacher prompting.
steps: Pause to celebrate routines performed correctly.
Review one routine that needs improvement.

blocks.subject: Writing Workshop
blocks.title: Lesson 2: Writing Materials
blocks.objective: Students identify, select, use, and return writing materials appropriately.
steps: Allow students to sample approved writing tools.

blocks.subject: Math
blocks.title: Math Launch and Partner Expectations
blocks.objective: Students learn math discussion, manipulatives, partner work, and cleanup routines.
steps: Model how to use and return manipulatives.
Use a selected activity from the Math PowerPoint.

blocks.subject: Assessment
blocks.title: Back-to-School Assessment Rotation
blocks.objective: Students demonstrate independent work expectations while the teacher begins required assessments.
steps: Explain what students do when finished.
Assess students individually or in small groups.
Use independent reading stamina or an approved activity for waiting students.

blocks.subject: Procedures
blocks.title: Materials, Partner Talk, Line-Up, and Hallway
blocks.objective: Students safely manage materials and transitions.
steps: Add procedures for supplies and partners.

prep: Organize writing tools for student sampling
Choose assessment students and independent tasks

days.focus: Choosing productive workspaces, solving small problems, and building stamina.

blocks.subject: Morning Work
blocks.title: Arrival Routine With Independence
blocks.objective: Students complete arrival and morning work with minimal reminders.

blocks.subject: Writing Workshop
blocks.title: Lesson 3: Finding Your Writing Place
blocks.objective: Students choose a productive writing location and practice writing stamina.
steps: Discuss what makes a good writing space.
Practice a short writing period and reflect.

blocks.subject: Math
blocks.title: Racing Facts Partner Game
blocks.objective: Students practice addition or subtraction facts while following partner-game expectations.
steps: Model rolling, solving, checking, and taking turns.

blocks.subject: Assessment
blocks.title: Continue Back-to-School Assessments
blocks.objective: Students sustain independent work while the teacher gathers baseline information.

blocks.subject: Procedures
blocks.title: Centers, Technology, Early Finishers, and Asking for Help
blocks.objective: Students know what to do without interrupting instruction.
steps: Teach how to ask for help.
Teach what to do when finished.

prep: Set up a short practice center

days.focus: Paper setup, collaborative planning, building, presenting, and cleanup.

blocks.subject: Writing Workshop
blocks.title: Lesson 4: Setting Up My Paper
blocks.objective: Students prepare writing paper with room for revision and editing.
steps: Explain why writers need revision space.
Model writing only on the front.
Model folding or leaving a revision column.
resources: Setting Up My Paper - Anchor Chart.pdf
Setting Up My Paper - Student Printable.pdf

blocks.subject: STEM and Community
blocks.title: Build a School Challenge
blocks.objective: Students collaborate to plan, construct, name, and present a model dream school.
steps: Reconnect to If I Built a School.
Allow ten minutes for sketching and planning.
resources: Read Aloud - If I Built a School.pdf

blocks.subject: Assessment
blocks.title: Complete Priority Back-to-School Assessments
blocks.objective: The teacher gathers additional baseline information while students work independently.

blocks.subject: Procedures
blocks.title: Group Work, Sharing Materials, Presenting, and Cleanup
blocks.objective: Students contribute responsibly to a collaborative project.

prep: Copy Setting Up My Paper printable
Prepare the anchor chart or PowerPoint
Prepare construction paper, glue, tape, straws, and pipe cleaners

days.focus: Solving classroom problems, telling time, reviewing procedures, and celebrating growth.

blocks.subject: Writing Workshop
blocks.title: Lesson 5: Solving Writing Workshop Problems
blocks.objective: Students independently solve common problems that occur during writing time.
steps: Create or review a problem-solving chart.
Practice using the solutions during work time.

blocks.subject: Math
blocks.title: Telling Time to the Hour and Half Hour
blocks.objective: Students tell and write time to the hour and half hour.
steps: Review the hour and minute hands.
Model writing time to the hour.
Model writing time to the half hour.
Use the PowerPoint for guided practice.
Complete the clock-domino activity in pairs or groups.

blocks.subject: Classroom Launch
blocks.title: Procedure Review and Student Demonstrations
blocks.objective: Students demonstrate the routines needed for core instruction to begin successfully.
steps: Celebrate progress and set a goal for Monday.

blocks.subject: Community
blocks.title: First-Week Reflection and Compliment Circle
blocks.objective: Students reflect on their learning, recognize classmates, and prepare for the beginning of core curriculum.
steps: Reflect on one routine students improved.

resourceCatalog.file: Read Aloud - If I Built a School.pdf
Setting Up My Paper - Anchor Chart.pdf
Setting Up My Paper - Student Printable.pdf
Personal Narrative Unit at a Glance.pdf
